steps.title: Identify what evidence would support the claim
steps.content: The researchers claim jaguars in the Pantanal can survive on more fish and aquatic reptiles than mammals. To directly support this claim, we need evidence showing what jaguars are actually eating.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the claim
steps.content: Choice A provides direct evidence: analysis of jaguar waste shows aquatic prey remains were detected more often than mammal remains, directly proving the claimed diet. The other three don't directly prove jaguars consume more aquatic prey: B shows only prey availability, C explains nutritional benefits without demonstrating consumption, and D indicates jaguars still eat mammals. That matches choice A — A is correct.
